rationale: The confirmed US-Iran peace deal announcement is the most significant event for US residents — oil prices falling and markets jumping represent a real, measurable economic change, while the deal itself is a major foreign policy development directly involving the US.

top_events.title: Oil prices fall and shares jump after US-Iran deal announced / US-Iran to sign a 'peace deal' on Friday
top_events.summary: The confirmed US-Iran peace deal announcement is already moving markets positively and pushing oil prices down — a real, measurable economic benefit for US consumers and investors, though the deal is not yet signed.

top_events.title: Trump says leader of Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang killed in US strike
top_events.summary: Confirmed killing of a Tren de Aragua gang leader in a US strike is a continuation of established domestic security operations — no qualitative new escalation for US residents.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic at conference for 'espionage activities'
top_events.summary: China detained another US academic on espionage charges — continuation of an established pattern of US-China tensions with no new qualitative escalation.
